business.headline: Sensex ends nearly 1,000 points up: 3 reasons why stock market jumped todaySensex and Nifty surged on Friday as IT stocks, Reliance Industries and private banks led buying. The S&P BSE Sensex was up 953.76 points, or 1.24%, at 78,140.63 around 3:07 pm, after touching an intraday high of 78,282.55.

8th Pay Commission: How much HRA could you get under different fitment factors?The HRA debate comes at an important stage in the 8th Pay Commission process. It has been more than seven months since the Commission was constituted. The Commission visited Kolkata, West Bengal, on July 9 and 10, 2026, as part of its ongoing consultation exercise.

Your ITR could be wrong even with Form 16: 7 smart checks to avoid tax noticesThink your Form 16 is all you need to file your ITR? Think again. A few missed details could delay your refund, or even land you a tax notice.

Maruti challenges landmark E20 ruling, says contaminated fuel was the real issueMaruti Suzuki will challenge the Raipur consumer court order on a Grand Vitara E20 dispute. The case sharpens scrutiny of whether alleged damage came from ethanol-blended petrol or contaminated fuel.

Why investors are betting against Elon Musk's SpaceXSome investors are concerned about the company's heavy investment in artificial intelligence, much of which is being financed through debt. These concerns have added pressure on the stock in recent weeks.

EPFO plans PF scheme for self-employed and unorganised workersIf implemented, the proposed scheme could significantly expand India's social security system by giving millions of self-employed individuals and workers in the unorganised sector an opportunity to save for retirement through a trusted and structured provident fund mechanism.

Trump posts, markets move. His company now wants to turn that into a businessTrump Media plans to launch Truth PSI, a paid service offering early access to Truth Social posts. The proposal has prompted criticism over possible market advantage and conflict-of-interest concerns.

Infosys, TCS, Tech Mahindra, HCLTech rally 2%; Why are IT stocks rising today?Tech Mahindra's stronger June-quarter earnings lifted IT shares, with Infosys, TCS and HCLTech joining the rally while Wipro fell. The divergent moves highlighted improving confidence in stronger performers as investors watched the sector's earnings season.

Tata, SP Group in fresh talks on Tata Sons stake swapTata Sons and the SP Group are exploring a share swap to monetise part of the latter's stake. The talks matter because they could reduce SP Group debt, though valuation and structure remain unresolved.

Markets open higher as IT stocks surge; Reliance rises before Q1 resultsThe S&P BSE Sensex climbed 456.40 points, or 0.59%, to 77,643.27, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 120.90 points, or 0.50%, to 24,193.65 as of 9:30 am.

SBI Funds Management IPO allotment: GMP at Rs 97. Here's how to check your statusSBI Funds Management IPO subscribed 41.73 times. Based on the upper price band of Rs 574 per share, the estimated listing price works out to around Rs 671, implying a potential listing gain of 16.90% if the current GMP trend holds.

Wipro hires 888 more employees in Q1. What's driving the headcount growth?Wipro's total employee count rose by 888 during the quarter ended June 30, taking its workforce to 243,044. This follows a period in which most large IT companies either slowed hiring or trimmed headcount amid weak global technology spending.

Wipro Q1 Results: Profit rises 1%, revenue up 11%; declares Rs 2 interim dividendWipro reported a consolidated net profit of Rs 3,352 crore for the quarter ended June 30, up 1% from Rs 3,330 crore in the same period last year.

Why the Rs 200 meal voucher tax benefit won't apply to your ITR this yearAs the ITR filing season gathers pace, our 'KNOW YOUR TAXES' series explains the tax rules that matter most. In this edition, we clear up one of the biggest misconceptions doing the rounds, whether you can claim the new Rs 200 per meal voucher tax benefit while filing your ITR this year.

Markets ends flat as banking drag offsets IT gains amid US-Iran tensionsThe S&P BSE Sensex ended almost unchanged, rising just 1.44 points to close at 77,186.87. The NSE Nifty50 slipped 5.75 points, or 0.02%, to settle at 24,072.75.

No micromanagement, no after-hours calls: Why an Indian engineer chose a UK firmAn Indian software engineer working remotely for a UK-based company has shared why he has stayed with his employer for more than five years, saying that while being paid in pounds is a nice perk, it isn't the biggest reason.

The hidden cost of renting a house in IndiaA report says tenants across six Indian metros have Rs 1.26 lakh crore locked in security deposits with landlords. The finding highlights how upfront deposits, refund risks and idle savings are reshaping the cost of renting.

Leaked Kudankulam plant data not linked to nuke systems: Nuclear power operatorIndia's nuclear power operator rejected reports of a sensitive data breach at the Kudankulam Nuclear Power Project, saying the leaked files relate only to conventional Balance of Plant facilities and not to any nuclear safety or security systems.

ITR filing alert: More than 5 crore taxpayers have checked this statement. Have you?With the ITR filing deadline nearing, taxpayers are busy gathering documents. But before you file your return, the Income Tax Department wants you to complete one simple step, one that over 5 crore taxpayers have already taken.

ICICI Lombard shares crash up to 15%: Why market investors are dumping the stockICICI Lombard reported a 46% year-on-year decline in net profit to Rs 403 crore for the quarter ended June, compared with Rs 747 crore in the corresponding period last year.

entertainment.headline: RRR echoes at New York City Hall as Zohran Mamdani presents Rental Rip-off report Zohran Mamdani referenced SS Rajamouli's RRR while unveiling New York's Rental Rip-off Report at City Hall. The remark drew laughs in the room and prompted a reaction from the film's official X handle.

Old Sonam Wangchuk video surfaces after Aamir Khan says he didn't know him in 2009 An old video of activist Sonam Wangchuk recalling his 2008 meeting with Aamir Khan has resurfaced. The clip showed up after the actor denied any connection between Wangchuk and his 3 Idiots character Phunsukh Wangdu.

Booked tickets in 2025, watched Nolan film in 2026: The Odyssey obsession is real The Odyssey was released today. Some fans had booked IMAX 70 mm first-day tickets a year ago. The frenzy, mirrored by themed costumes and steep advance sales in India, underlined filmmaker Christopher Nolan's draw.

They saw what happened to me: Swara Bhasker on Bollywood skipping CJP protest At Jantar Mantar, Swara Bhasker said many in Bollywood avoided Sonam Wangchuk's protest out of fear. Her remarks came as Wangchuk's hunger strike entered a critical phase and renewed questions over public support.

The Odyssey ticket prices soar in India, premium seats cost up to Rs 3,400 Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey is racing through advance bookings in India ahead of its July 17 release. Premium pricing, rave reviews and trade forecasts have set up a likely opening above Oppenheimer.

Why India, why now? The real reason behind Nolan bringing The Odyssey to India Director Christopher Nolan brought actors Matt Damon and Tom Holland to Mumbai for The Odyssey's India premiere. The visit underscored India's rising importance in the film's global campaign and India as a potential market for Nolan's films.

BTS' NORMAL is a quiet reflection on fame, friendship and finding normalcy BTS has unveiled their much-anticipated NORMAL music video, wrapping up an innovative teaser campaign that captured global attention. The understated release reframes fame through friendship, silence, and the search for ordinary moments.

Mahaprabhu Jagannath gets Supreme Court nod, release after Rath Yatra The Supreme Court allowed the release of Mahaprabhu Jagannath after Rath Yatra rituals end. The order balances CBFC clearance with objections over scenes said to depart from Puranic tradition.

BTS say ARIRANG reunion proves the group still comes first BTS has once again proven that their group identity remains at the heart of all they do, even after years of solo projects and military service. Their recent ARIRANG tour and documentary beautifully capture this spirit, showing fans across the world the unbreakable bond of seven members performing as one.

The Odyssey X review: Nolan's epic floors fans, but they want better IMAX screens Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey has triggered a flood of gushing reactions on X, with viewers praising its scale, pacing and performances. The early mood is lavishly positive, framing the film as both a big-screen spectacle and a moving meditation on war, family and home.

Dheeraj Dhoopar is not boring on Lock Upp, best is yet to come: Wife Vinny Arora Actor Vinny Arora said her husband Dheeraj Dhoopar is simply being himself on Lock Upp. She dismissed criticism that he is 'boring' or playing safe. Vinny also opened up about his biggest struggle on the show and why the family was worried before he signed up.

Why censor board removed Jana Nayagan's Ambedkar-TVK dialogue? Producer explains Jana Nayagan producer K Venkata Narayana said that the censor board sought changes to a dialogue mentioning Dr BR Ambedkar and TVK. His remarks clarify the board's objections after months of certification delays and revisions.

Kiran Rao backs Sonam Wangchuk as Aamir Khan denies 3 Idiots role was based on him Kiran Rao joined public figures in urging Sonam Wangchuk to end his fast and asking the Union government to open talks on his demands. The appeal came as his NEET-linked protest entered Day 20, drawing wider support and rising concern over his health.

Arjun Bijlani defends 15-year friendship bond with Mouni Roy, slams dating buzz Arjun Bijlani dismissed rumours linking him with longtime friend Mouni Roy after their dinner outing sparked dating speculation. The actor slammed gossip pages for creating false narratives and urged media platforms to verify facts before publishing.

Aamir Khan says his 3 Idiots role not based on Wangchuk amid his hunger strike Aamir Khan said Phunsukh Wangdu in 3 Idiots was not based on activist Sonam Wangchuk. His remarks revived the old debate even as concern grew over Wangchuk's 20-day hunger strike.

Kim Kardashian defends post after grandmother's death, Lewis Hamilton backs her Kim Kardashian clarified that her lakeside holiday post had been scheduled before the death of her grandmother, Mary Jo 'MJ' Shannon, after facing online backlash. Hours later, Lewis Hamilton shared a heartfelt message extending support.

The Odyssey, Ramayana and Mahabharata: The similarities you never noticed Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey is being framed through parallels with the Ramayana and the Mahabharata. The comparison rests on shared themes of exile, temptation, divine intervention and homecoming, even as the epics remain culturally distinct.

Mary Jo MJ Shannon dies at 91: Kris Jenner mourns, Kim Kardashian shares tribute Kris Jenner announced that her mother, Mary Jo 'MJ' Shannon, has died at 91, prompting tributes from across the Kardashian family. Their messages remembered MJ as the family's emotional centre and a lasting influence on their values and work ethic.

Tom Cruise raves about Nolan's The Odyssey, praises brilliant cast and crew Actor Tom Cruise praised Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey after attending a late-night screening and sharing a premiere photo on social media. His post highlighted his enthusiasm for the theatrical experience ahead of the film's worldwide release.

general news.headline: Will return as a ghost: Sonam Wangchuk gives July 20th condition for ending fastActivist Sonam Wangchuk, whose indefinite hunger strike entered its 20th day, has announced when he would end his fast. Wangchuk said he would end the fast on July 20 after participating in the march to Parliament. He joked that he would 'come back as a ghost' if there wasn't much participation in the march.

India to blame for Bullet Train project delay, says former Japanese ministerFormer Japanese minister Hideki Makihara accused Indian authorities over the alleged delay in the Mumbai-Ahmedabad Bullet Train project. The minister in-charge of the negotiations was especially awful, alleged Makihara. Indian officials have junked the allegations.

Rohit Sharma's retirement speculation explained: Indian cricket set for messy weekendRohit Sharma's dream of playing one last ODI World Cup appears to have been cut short. Reports have emerged out of England that the selectors have decided to move on from the former India captain. But with Shubman Gill publicly backing Rohit just days earlier, Indian cricket finds itself caught in another messy episode of mixed messaging.

Iran claims it struck US fighter jets with missiles and drones in JordanIran claimed it has destroyed US fighter jets and refuelling aircraft stationed in Jordan in retaliatory strikes after American attacks, as the widening conflict fuels fresh fears of regional escalation.

When a death after 58-day hunger strike shook Nehru, gave birth to AndhraFive years after Independence, Gandhian leader and activist Potti Sriramulu began a fast until death demanding a separate state for Telugu-speaking people. His death after 58 days of fast forced PM Jawaharlal Nehru to act and declare the formation of the Andhra State, the first on linguistic lines.

Mahesh Tutorials shuts 33 coaching centres in Maharashtra; 2,500 students affectedMahesh Tutorials has abruptly shut 33 coaching centres across Maharashtra, affecting around 2,500 students after admissions and fee collection for the academic year. The closure comes amid the long-running insolvency proceedings of parent company MT Educare, leaving students, parents and staff facing uncertainty over classes, fees and salaries.

India gets first hydrogen train: If it needs no electricity, how does it run?India has launched its first indigenously developed hydrogen fuel cell train on the Jind-Sonipat section. The pilot builds railway infrastructure and operational experience for wider green mobility.

Why are top scientists leaving Isro? Money is not the only issueMore than 100 scientists have resigned or taken voluntary retirement from Isro, prompting the Department of Space to tighten exit rules for staff on flagship missions.

Old Sonam Wangchuk video surfaces after Aamir Khan says he didn't know him in 2009An old video of activist Sonam Wangchuk recalling his 2008 meeting with Aamir Khan has resurfaced. The clip showed up after the actor denied any connection between Wangchuk and his 3 Idiots character Phunsukh Wangdu.

Live: Tom Cruise cheers for Nolan's filmThe Odyssey Movie Review and Release Live Updates: The wait is finally over! Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey has sailed into theatres, and the internet is already calling it an epic for the ages. Follow our live blog for fan reactions, box office updates, social media buzz and every major highlight from Nolan's epic.

Paani wali rail chala di: How Haryana BJP is selling India's first hydrogen trainIndia's first indigenous hydrogen train, launched by PM Narendra Modi in Haryana on Friday, is a technological milestone. The Haryana BJP pitched it as a "paani waali rail". While it's inaccurate, it's definitely a more direct and relatable messaging.

Aamir Khan says his 3 Idiots role not based on Wangchuk amid his hunger strikeAamir Khan said Phunsukh Wangdu in 3 Idiots was not based on activist Sonam Wangchuk. His remarks revived the old debate even as concern grew over Wangchuk's 20-day hunger strike.

What if Lionel Messi had played for Spain?Long before Lionel Messi became the face of Argentine football, Spain believed it had a chance to make him its own. As Argentina and Spain prepare to meet in the World Cup final, one of football's great what-ifs returns to the spotlight: what if Messi had chosen Spain?

Forward Deployed Engineers are in demand but what is FDE and how do you become one?With the AI boom, one tech job has risen in demand – Forward Deployed Engineers, or FDEs. Be it OpenAI, Google or even TCS, everyone wants FDEs and are willing to pay big money for them. But how do you become one?

No business writing doctor: Supreme Court's sharp rap in 4-year-old's rape caseThe Supreme Court on Friday rebuked two private hospitals over allegations they refused treatment to a four-year-old rape victim in Ghaziabad. The child later died.

NDA Parliament Party meet will now be called 'Mangal Milan' from Monsoon SessionThe NDA has renamed its July 21 parliamentary party meeting in Parliament House as Mangal Milan. The change comes as the government prepares to prioritise key ordinances in the Monsoon Session and signals confidence in its numbers.

Fashionably late, ruthlessly on time: Argentina make comebacks a habitFor the third straight knockout game, Argentina waited until the dying moments to land the decisive blow. Their dramatic comeback against England was far more than just Lionel Messi's brilliance as it was another reminder of a team that thrives when the pressure is at its highest.

Anurag Kumar appointed Delhi Police Commissioner, replacing Satish GolchaA 1994-batch IPS officer, Anurag Kumar served as Special Director in the Intelligence Bureau (IB) before his appointment as the Delhi Police chief.

Pakistan eyes fifth province status for Gilgit-Baltistan amid turmoil in POKGilgit-Baltistan Assembly unanimously passed a resolution seeking constitutional recognition and representation in the Pakistan Parliament, a move India rejects as it considers the region an integral part of Jammu and Kashmir.

Jantar Mantar chhhoo mantar: How Wangchuk transformed a circus into a protestThe Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) might have been born online, but its identity is now being shaped at New Delhi's Jantar Mantar. Nineteen days into his indefinite hunger strike, Sonam Wangchuk has become much more than just a protester. For many, he is now the face, conscience and symbol of the movement.

The Odyssey review: A timeless epic reborn through Christopher Nolan's geniusRelease Date:Fri Jul 17, 2026The Odyssey movie review: Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey blends myth, emotion and breathtaking spectacle into one unforgettable journey. Anchored by Matt Damon's brilliant performance, it's a film that demands to be seen on the biggest screen.

I spent a night as a cockroach at CJP's Jantar Mantar protest. This is what I sawI stayed overnight at the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) protest site at New Delhi's Jantar Mantar without revealing my identity. I spoke to the participants, ate the food being served and overheard the conversations around fasting activist Sonam Wangchuk. I also noticed the unease over absent CJP leaders.

India's Agnikul joins global space race for reusable rockets, unveils Mission 02Agnikul Cosmos has announced Mission-02 to recover an orbital-class rocket booster and repurpose its upper stage in orbit. The mission would advance reusable launch technology in India as private space companies push lower-cost access to space.

One's dream came from loss, another's from love for biology: Journey of NEET toppersNEET UG 2026 toppers Aryan Gupta and Panshul Bansal prove there is no single formula for success. One was inspired by personal loss, the other by a love for biology, but both reached the top through discipline, consistency and unwavering focus.

sports.headline: Former India U-19 star Manjot Kalra arrested in Lanka Premier League bribery case Former India Under-19 World Cup winner Manjot Kalra was arrested in Sri Lanka over allegations of attempting to bribe players during the Lanka Premier League, where he is a co-owner of the Jaffna Kings franchise.

The quiet captain: Why Lionel Messi's leadership merits an MBA case study For years, Lionel Messi was judged by the one trophy that eluded him. Today, with another World Cup final on the horizon, the conversation has shifted from what he failed to win to how he transformed a team, a nation and the very idea of leadership.

2nd T20I: Live Score and Updates from Bulawayo ZIM vs BAN, 2nd T20I: Zimbabwe have won the toss and opted to bowl first in Bulawayo. The hosts are unchanged after winning the series opener while Bangladesh have brought in Rishad Hossain for Nasum Ahmed.

No surprises as India bank on proven names for Women's Hockey World Cup 2026 India have retained the same 20-player squad for the Women's Hockey World Cup, with Salima Tete to captain the side. India have opted for a perfect blend for youth and experience as they head to the Netherlands and Belgium.

Meet Keyne Yamal: Lamine's younger brother stealing the World Cup spotlight While Lamine Yamal prepares to face Lionel Messi in the World Cup final, his younger brother Keyne has become Spain's breakout figure from the stands. The three-year-old's antics and obvious bond with Lamine have made him the team's unofficial mascot during the tournament.

Pakistan's Mohammad Nawaz penalised after ICC anti-doping code breach Pakistan all-rounder Mohammad Nawaz has accepted a sanction after testing positive for Carboxy-THC during the 2026 T20 World Cup, with his three-month ban set to be reduced upon completion of a treatment programme.

Fashionably late, ruthlessly on time: Argentina make comebacks a habit For the third straight knockout game, Argentina waited until the dying moments to land the decisive blow. Their dramatic comeback against England was far more than just Lionel Messi's brilliance as it was another reminder of a team that thrives when the pressure is at its highest.

Parthiv Patel backs Rohit Sharma to bow out in style amid retirement talk Former India wicketkeeper Parthiv Patel backed Rohit Sharma to silence his critics with a century at Lord's amid growing speculation that the series decider against England could be his final ODI appearance.

Bellingham shares team bus driver's letter with England fans after WC exit Jude Bellingham thanked England supporters after the World Cup semi-final loss to Argentina, sharing a handwritten note from the team bus driver. Bellingham promised the fans that the team would bounce back and win major tournaments in the future.

Rohit Sharma's retirement speculation explained: Indian cricket set for messy weekend Rohit Sharma's dream of playing one last ODI World Cup appears to have been cut short. Reports have emerged out of England that the selectors have decided to move on from the former India captain. But with Shubman Gill publicly backing Rohit just days earlier, Indian cricket finds itself caught in another messy episode of mixed messaging.

Vincic, referee for World Cup final, has a shocking past with Messi, Argentina FIFA has appointed Slavko Vincic to referee the World Cup final between Argentina and Spain. But the announcement will have the Albiceleste fans sweating as Vincic was the official for one of Argentina's shocking losses in recent times in the tournament.

PV Sindhu ends India's 15-year wait, reaches maiden Japan Open semisPV Sindhu reached her maiden Japan Open semi-final after Nozomi Okuhara withdrew from their quarter-final clash, ending India's 15-year wait for a women's singles semi-final appearance at the Super 750 event.

What if Lionel Messi had played for Spain?Long before Lionel Messi became the face of Argentine football, Spain believed it had a chance to make him its own. As Argentina and Spain prepare to meet in the World Cup final, one of football's great what-ifs returns to the spotlight: what if Messi had chosen Spain?

Carlos Alcaraz finally returns to action, named in start list for Cincinnati OpenCarlos Alcaraz is set to return to the ATP Tour after a four-month injury layoff, having been named in the Cincinnati Open start list ahead of the US Open.

Watch: Slovenian referee moved to tears after World Cup final appointmentSlovenia's Slavko Vincic was appointed referee for the World Cup final between Argentina and Spain in New York. His tearful reaction to the announcement underlined what the assignment means beyond the touchline.

Smriti Mandhana ready for India captaincy. But is she good enough?In an exclusive interview with India Today, former India fast bowler Devika Palshikar weighed in on whether Smriti Mandhana possesses the qualities and leadership skills needed to captain the national team at the highest level.

No pressure on Rohit: Team India make stance clear as retirement buzz growsSitanshu Kotak said India are not putting Rohit Sharma under pressure despite the noise around his ODI future. Reports on Thursday suggested that the selectors are ready to move on from Rohit and he wasn't in the plans for the ODI World Cup next year.

No rift between Kohli and Gambhir: India batting coach slams rumoursIndia batting coach Sitanshu Kotak said Virat Kohli and Gautam Gambhir were in regular conversation during the second ODI in Cardiff and dismissed rumours suggesting that there was a rift in the working relationship between the two.

2nd ODI match report: Joe Root's 99 powers England to series-levelling win vs IndiaEngland vs India, 2nd ODI match report: Joe Root was left stranded on 99 not out as England beat India by four wickets in the second ODI at Sophia Gardens in Cardiff on Thursday to level the three-match series 1-1.

Trump to attend World Cup final between Argentina and Spain: White HouseThe White House has confirmed that US President Donald Trump will attend the FIFA World Cup final between Argentina and Spain on Sunday, with FIFA president Gianni Infantino set to join him in presenting the trophy to the winners.

Ex-India captain wants Mandhana to takeover ODI, Test side from HarmanpreetMithali Raj has backed Smriti Mandhana to succeed Harmanpreet Kaur as India's ODI and Test captain, while calling for Shafali Varma to be groomed as the T20I skipper. The former India captain said the selectors must look beyond one ICC event and build a long-term leadership roadmap.

Yamal injury scare? Spain star trains alone before World Cup final vs ArgentinaLamine Yamal and Pedro Porro trained separately as Spain resumed preparations for the World Cup final against Argentina. The federation said the move was precautionary, easing concerns before Sunday's title clash.

Will Rohit Sharma retire? Lord's ODI could be his last match for IndiaThe third ODI against England at Lord's on Sunday could be Rohit Sharma's final match for India, with the national selection committee unlikely to retain the veteran opener beyond the ongoing UK tour, according to a PTI report.

ENG vs IND: Rohit Sharma fails but Virat Kohli shines with fluent 65 in CardiffEngland vs India, 2nd ODI: Virat Kohli and Rohit Sharma have carried India's batting for more than a decade. But the second ODI against England in Cardiff showed the two veterans in very different touch.

Kranti Gaud felicitated after scripting history in India's Lord's Test victoryKranti Gaud was felicitated by Madhya Pradesh Sports Minister Vishwas Kailash Sarang on Thursday after playing a pivotal role in India's historic Test victory over England at Lord's.

Why is KL Rahul not playing the second ODI vs England? India reveal reasonIndia made one change to their playing XI for the second ODI against England, with wicketkeeper-batter Ishan Kishan replacing the unwell KL Rahul after England won the toss and elected to field first.

ENG vs IND: Gurnoor Brar gets penalised by ICC after Ben Duckett spat in 1st ODIGurnoor Brar was officially warned by the ICC after throwing the ball near Ben Duckett during the first ODI in Birmingham. The sanction brought one demerit point for the Brar, who accepted responsibility for the mistake.

UK government tells FIFA to probe Argentina over Falklands banner celebrationThe UK has asked FIFA to examine Argentina players displaying a Falklands claim banner after beating England. Argentina defeated the Three Lions 2-1 in Atlanta to reach the final.

Peter Drury of backwaters: Malayalam commentary on Messi magic goes viralCelebrated Malayalam commentator Shaiju Damodaran's call amplified Lionel Messi's celebrations after Argentina beat England in the semi-final. The clip was widely shared on social media on Thursday.

Thomas Tuchel got it wrong but England's biggest problem is the noise around themThomas Tuchel's tactical retreat cost England against Argentina, but another World Cup exit exposed a deeper issue. From relentless scrutiny to former players creating needless narratives, the noise around the Three Lions may be hurting them more than they realise.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
